name: Ping Pong
current_goal: Maintain the single-node intranet speed-test service at app version 0.3.0 with browser-scoped Recent Results, fixed homepage Quick 20s and Full 30s modes, proportional startup trimming, Raw CV and Stable CV throughput diagnostics, IQR kept/outlier-rate card diagnostics, current-run Raw Data popup/CSV diagnostics, a topbar English speed-test guide dialog, stability-first completion summaries, hotspot data-plan warning, required per-test Wired/Wi-Fi labeling, current connection IP visibility, Web Worker execution, HTML/PNG/Markdown IT diagnostic report export, local-throttled maintenance self-tests, a desktop one-screen dashboard, Admin Console controls, local production health, OctoPulse visibility, and repo-visible reusable documentation.
latest_summary: Added a viewport-overflow fallback so the homepage keeps one-screen desktop layout when it fits and switches to normal scrolling before cards overlap on shorter or zoomed viewports.
next_action: Review and commit the homepage layout fallback updates in `src/client/App.tsx`, `src/client/styles.css`, `README.md`, and `.ai/status.json`.

last_commands: Browser rendered layout verification at 1089x964, 1280x900, 1710x1021, and 1280x760
verification.notes: Typecheck, full Vitest suite (97 tests), whitespace diff check for the edited client files, and a production build all passed. The PORT=8200 launchd service was restarted after the build; port 8200 was listening and `/api/health` returned ok. Browser verification at 1089x964, 1280x900, and 1710x1021 kept the one-screen desktop layout without metric-card overlap, while 1280x760 switched to the scroll-safe fallback class before cards could collide with each other or panel borders. Browser console warnings/errors were empty during the rendered checks.

attention: Working tree is dirty because this layout fix updates `src/client/App.tsx`, `src/client/styles.css`, `README.md`, and `.ai/status.json`.
The local branch currently tracks origin/main.
The running service uses .env PORT=8200, not the README default 8080.
The desktop layout now keeps one-screen mode only when the rendered content fits; shorter or zoomed viewports should scroll instead of overlapping cards.
Local throttled runs are maintenance checks only; a non-local intranet device test is still required to evaluate real network performance.

recent_changes.summary: Added a rendered-height fallback so desktop keeps one-screen mode only when the homepage fits and otherwise scrolls instead of overlapping cards.
